name: correction-trail
title: Memory leak investigation with correction trail

items.content: Memory leak in worker pool goroutines — heap grows ~50MB/hour under sustained load. Suspected goroutine leak in pool shutdown path.
Add context cancellation propagation to all worker goroutines so they exit cleanly on pool shutdown.
ctx.Done() is already checked in the worker loop — goroutines do exit on cancellation. Leak is elsewhere.
CORRECTION of finding 0: root cause is NOT goroutine leak in worker pool. Actual cause: HTTP client not closing response bodies — deferred resp.Body.Close() missing in error return paths.
resp.Body.Close() missing on early return in error path of doRequest(). Body accumulates in memory until GC collects unreachable transport connections.
Always defer resp.Body.Close() immediately after nil error check — before any further processing. Applied to all call sites in src/http/.
Fix applied. Goroutine count stable at ~120 under sustained load for 2 hours. Heap growth flat. pprof heap profile shows no accumulation.
PR #401 merged to main. CI green. No regressions in 48h canary deployment.
Cherry-pick fix commit to release/2.4 — customers on stable channel affected. Patch release 2.4.1 planned.
